problem_statement: The paper addresses how to safely automate LLM-driven refactoring of data clumps in Git repositories, where LLMs may produce faulty or unreliable refactorings, requiring human oversight to ensure trustworthiness and regulatory compliance.
magnitude_evidence: maintenance costs 'often constitute a significant portion, ranging from 40% to 80% of total expenses'
insufficiency_evidence: 'The refactored code by the LLM must be modified by a human-in-the-loop before it could compile'; median combined detect+refactor score of GPT-3.5-Turbo was 7% vs 82% for GPT-4
solution_mechanisms: feedback loop of rejected refactorings into next iteration
validation via build tool / CI test execution
AI-assistance transparency disclosure to repo owners
governance_refs: EU Ethics Guidelines for Trustworthy AI
